title: Lankesteriana casualis
intro: Lankesteriana casualis, also known as the Casaba orchid, is a type of flowering orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits, such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lankesteriana casualis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
